title: monticalia schultzei subsp. sevillana
intro: Monticalia schultzei subsp. sevillana, also known as the Andean tree fern, is a sub-species of plant in the Asteropeiaceae family, native to the Andean region of South America. The medicinal properties of Monticalia schultzei subsp. sevillana include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. This plant has been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, skin irritations, and respiratory infections, providing benefits such as reduced inflammation, improved wound healing, and enhanced immune function. The herbal preparations of Monticalia schultzei subsp. sevillana include teas, infusions, and topical applications, often used to soothe and calm the body.
